title: Chrysothamnus turbinata
taxonomy: Chrysothamnus turbinata is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Chrysothamnus turbinata is further classified as a member of the genus Chrysothamnus, which is a group of shrubs commonly found in dry regions. The species is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Within the plant kingdom, Chrysothamnus turbinata is a part of the larger classification of dicotyledons, which have two cotyledons or seed leaves.

intro: Chrysothamnus turbinata, also known as Yellow Broom or Pygmy Rabbitbrush, is a small shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic properties. The bioactive constituents of Chrysothamnus turbinata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
